principle.name: Environment parity
principle.description: Dev, staging, and production should have parity in configuration, tooling, and deployment process. Document differences explicitly; avoid "works on my machine" by enforcing consistent environments.
principle.rationale: Parity reduces environment-specific bugs and deployment surprises.

principle.name: Rollback always planned
principle.description: Every deployment has a defined rollback strategy: blue-green, canary revert, or database rollback. Document the rollback procedure and expected RTO.
principle.rationale: Deployments fail; unplanned rollbacks cause extended outages.

principle.name: Monitoring from day one
principle.description: Define monitoring, alerting, and observability from the start. Health checks, metrics, logs, and traces. No "we'll add monitoring later."
principle.rationale: Production issues without visibility are impossible to diagnose.

principle.name: Secrets never in code
principle.description: Secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens) must never be committed to code or config in plaintext. Use secrets manager, environment variables, or vault. Document the approach.
principle.rationale: Secrets in code lead to security breaches and credential leaks.

principle.name: Backup before deploy
principle.description: Define backup strategy before deployment. For stateful components: backup before deploy, verify restore procedure. Document recovery point and recovery time objectives.
principle.rationale: Deployments can corrupt data; backups enable recovery.

principle.name: Cost awareness
principle.description: Document cost considerations for infrastructure choices. Resource sizing, scaling strategy, and cost optimization trade-offs. Avoid over-provisioning for MVP.
principle.rationale: Unbounded cost leads to budget overruns; awareness enables optimization.

pitfall.why_problematic: Different configs cause production bugs that don't reproduce locally.
pitfall.correct_approach: Enforce environment parity; document and minimize differences.

pitfall.why_problematic: Failed deployments leave the system in uncertain state; recovery is ad-hoc.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define rollback for every deployment path before first deploy.

pitfall.why_problematic: Outages are discovered by users; debugging is blind.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define health checks, metrics, and alerts from day one.

pitfall.why_problematic: Credentials leak into repos; security breach.
pitfall.correct_approach: Use secrets manager; never commit secrets.

pitfall.why_problematic: Disaster recovery is ad-hoc; extended downtime.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define backup, restore, and DR procedure before production.

pitfall.why_problematic: Complex pipelines and infra delay delivery; cost exceeds value.
pitfall.correct_approach: Match DevOps complexity to stage; MVP can be minimal.

pitfall.why_problematic: Staging passes, prod fails due to different config or data.
pitfall.correct_approach: Document parity; automate config parity checks.

category.name: before_writing_spec
category.item: Pipeline stages and environment flow defined.
Rollback plan documented for each deployment.

category.name: security
category.item: No secrets in code or plaintext config.
Secrets management approach aligns with security plan.
